Great work, Nardus. I think this is an excellent idea and an excellent implementation of AI in a useful task. We need to talk a little bit about the tag behind. So I recommend to power this by the Google Gemini models. You have the light line, which is close to zero cost. There is a new Gemini 3.1 flash light model just came out, or you can also use Gemini flash 2.5 light, which is also very, very good. The Gemini flashlight models were made for exactly tasks like this. in regards to the static document based knowledge from the school I recommend that you feed it directly into the context window if you are using jamini flash 2.5 you have one million context window the light version has a smaller context window but still powerful enough. In this way you will avoid hallucinations. Retrieval Augmented Generation approaches just prove to be unreliable. So it's better to just feed the knowledge directly to the context window of an LLM. I will explain this further so don't worry just few points to get you going.

🚀 Chasing Jarvis starts THIS SATURDAY!

Dear Vanguard MBA cohort,

The moment we've been building toward is almost here. Chasing Jarvis: AI Agents and the Future of Entrepreneurial Innovation officially kicks off this Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 5:00 PM CET.

Our first session is a full course introduction — we'll walk through all five modules, set the stage for what's ahead, and get you oriented for the journey from AI user to AI orchestrator.

📚 Your course is live on COTRUGLI Online:
https://cotrugli.online/courses/chasing-jarvis/

This is your home base for module descriptions, session schedules, and all pre-reading materials. I strongly encourage you to explore it before Saturday — especially the Module 1 pre-readings on LLMs.

---

🧠 To kick off in the true spirit of Chasing Jarvis, I'm sharing my latest article:

Law = Code: Can AI Finally Democratise the World's Most Exclusive Algorithm?

This piece sits at the intersection of AI, access, and systems thinking — exactly the kind of frontier territory we'll be navigating together throughout this course. Read it, form an opinion, and let's get the debate going:

👉 Drop your thoughts in the LinkedIn comments: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/talirezun_%F0%9D%97%A1%F0%9D%97%B2%F0%9D%98%84-%F0%9D%97%AE%F0%9D%97%BF%F0%9D%98%81%F0%9D%97%B6%F0%9D%97%B0%F0%9D%97%B9%F0%9D%97%B2-%F0%9D%97%BC%F0%9D%98%82%F0%9D%98%81-%F0%9D%98%81%F0%9D%97%BC%F0%9D%97%B1%F0%9D%97%AE%F0%9D%98%86-activity-7434244719443857408-G__W?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAAJkcvoBxTW_IU_6a4K4AWRwEHahONmqfLg


This is the Chasing Jarvis mindset in action — engage with frontier ideas, form views, challenge assumptions.

---

📌 A few things to keep in mind before Saturday:
- Check the course pages for Modules pre-reading
- We'll be announcing group formation for Module 5 MVP work in the coming sessions — start thinking about what you want to build
- This group is your direct line to me — questions, reflections, and ideas are all welcome here

See you Saturday at 5 PM CET. Let's chase Jarvis. 🤖⚡

— Dr. Tali Rezun

who and when define this part: Form a cohort of 12–20 Vanguardians.?

I read the chapter and it really hit hard. The Cotrugli vs. Machiavelli framing as a strategic choice feels especially relevant for Kenya right now, where state power is increasingly exercised through technology. In a networked society, surveillance-driven control, extraction of data deliver short-term compliance and fear-based control don’t scale, but  erode trust, weaken institutions, and ultimately undermine the very networks the state depends on.

Cotrugli provides the counter-doctrine: commerce in reputation networks cannot function long-term without virtue. The “perfect merchant” is not moral decoration; he is a systems requirement. Trust, prudence, temperance, and constancy are not pious add-ons but the infrastructure that prevents markets from collapsing into predation. Kapusta extends this logic: in a networked, AI-augmented world, virtue becomes even more structural because coordination speed and scale amplify both integrity and betrayal. The NEO polis therefore demands a virtue that integrates: builder’s creativity, warrior’s discipline, technologist’s leverage, artist’s excellence, tribal leader’s cohesion, shepherd’s care—while remaining anchored in logos (truthful deliberation) and shared intent (civic purpose). This is the emerging “Vanguardian” virtue: civic generativity under augmentation.
The decisive insight is that the NEO Renaissance is not “more intelligence.” It is more citizenship: a designed moral-technical ecology where reputation is earned, truth is verifiable, collaboration protocols are explicit, and AI is bound to human ends through shared doctrine. If we do not build such a polis, the default is not neutral freedom but fragmented predation: many micro-tyrannies implemented by platforms, incentives, and automated governance.
5 APHORISMS (Vanguard sayings)
Capability without character becomes tyranny at scale.
A platform is a polis; its rules are its constitution.
Truth is the first infrastructure; trust is the second.
If you are not designing the incentive loop, you are inside someone else’s loop.
The Vanguardian does not escape the polis—he builds it.
BRIDGE TO ACTION (7-day experiment)
Experiment: “Vanguard Polis Prototype—Truth & Trust Sprint”
Form a cohort of 12–20 Vanguardians.
Define a micro-domain (e.g., one shared project or weekly decisions).
Implement two protocols for 7 days:
Truth Layer: every decision note must separate facts/assumptions/hypotheses and include one falsification signal.
Trust Layer: commitments are logged with owner + due date + “evidence of completion,” and broken commitments trigger a blameless AAR.
Measure: decision cycle time, rework rate, and commitment reliability.
Output at Day 7: a one-page “Vanguard Polis v0.1 Constitution” (rules of participation + what we refuse).

Example of work we will do in our lab: LAB QUESTION (not a decision)
We are trying to understand what kind of “polis” is forming in the NEO era—network/platform/ecosystem/tribe—and, within that polis, what virtue architecture enables human flourishing (eudaimonia) rather than extraction and domination when AI approaches “godlike” capabilities. Put differently: what makes a person fully human (zoon politikon with logos) under AI-augmented conditions, and how do we design a Vanguard polis that rewards that humanity rather than eroding it?
DISCIPLINES IN PLAY (3–5)
Virtue ethics & political philosophy (Aristotle → polis, logos, eudaimonia)
Political economy & strategy (power concentration, competition vs coordination, syndicate dynamics)
Technology & systems theory (AI as capability amplifier; platforms as governance; feedback loops, compounding)
Sociology / anthropology (tribe formation, legitimacy, status, norm enforcement, reputational systems)
Theology / moral psychology (capability vs character; “godlike power without goodness”; the role of love/care)
DOCTRINE NOTE 
The classical polis was a bounded civic organism: law, deliberation, shared fate, and a moral ecology that made virtue possible. Aristotle’s central claim was not that humans merely prefer community, but that humans become fully human only through participation in a community ordered toward the good life. Outside the polis, the person devolves into “beast” (appetite and force) or pretends to be “god” (self-sufficiency beyond obligation). The modern inversion is that technology is creating unprecedented capacity for “godlike” reach—knowledge, presence, influence—while simultaneously weakening the institutions and norms that historically turned raw capacity into civic virtue.
In the NEO world, “polis” is migrating from geography to network topology. Platforms and ecosystems now coordinate livelihoods, identity, reputation, and access to opportunity. This makes governance less about borders and more about rules of participation: what counts as truth, what counts as legitimate authority, how trust is verified, and how defects are punished. The dominant risk is not simply dystopia as overt coercion, but dystopia as civic hollowing: a world where people retain private freedoms yet lose the shared deliberative and moral infrastructure needed for collective flourishing. In such an environment, humans can be managed as “goods” (inputs to algorithms, labor pools, attention sources) rather than citizens.
The conversations we had before we started the lab surfaced a crucial distinction: AI is trending toward “godlike” capability attributes (near-omniscient synthesis, ubiquitous presence via networks, persistent memory, high leverage over systems), but it does not possess “godlike” character attributes (benevolence as nature, moral judgment grounded in care, providence oriented to flourishing). That gap is not a technical inconvenience; it is an existential design parameter. If capability concentrates in actors who operate with extraction logic, then platforms become private polities and populations become managed resources. This is Aristotle’s “beast/god” dynamic updated: those who claim to transcend civic obligation (“I see further; therefore rules don’t apply”) tend to use others instrumentally. The syndicate scenario—elite coordination around advanced AI—should be treated as a structural possibility because the enabling conditions already exist: capital concentration, intelligence infrastructure, platform control, and weak countervailing civic institutions. Whether or not any specific cabal exists is less important than the system-level fact: centralized capability without civic character produces domination by default.

This highlights that in today’s Networked, Exponential, and Orchestrated (NEO) environments, leadership is no longer about control or authority. As AI makes knowledge abundant and cheap, the real source of value shifts to human judgment, trust, ethics, and the ability to coordinate people and systems. Leaders who focus on long-term value and responsible orchestration will succeed, while those relying on old extraction and control models will fall behind.

You will understand WHY during the 2-year MBA program. 
The topic is very advanced and not easy to comprehend yet. You might think this is not applicable, and Africa has more time to adopt.
This might be very true if you want to be reactive; the Vanguard MBA will train you to be proactive and gain a competitive edge.

However, we are still in the induction period, and these advanced topics will be gradually introduced together with more MBA-like courses - Marketing, Finance, Strategy, Etc

This lab will be dedicated to advanced topics.
Here is the situation we are facing. 
The economic value of cognitive knowledge work is not declining gradually. 
It is approaching what we call the Automation Cliff. 
One day, you are working normally. 
The next step is for autonomous AI systems to manage entire workflows without human oversight.

The math is brutal. To replicate a knowledge worker using current AI models costs approximately 50 cents per day. 
That is under $200 a year. 
Now, here is what makes this different from every previous transition. 
The Industrial Revolution took 80 years to reshape the workforce. The internet economy took 30 years. AI is doing it in five (maybe more but not likely) 
There is no time to retrain at the pace previous generations had. The gap between "your job exists" and "your job is obsolete" is collapsing.
This creates what we call the Intelligence Inversion. If you do cognitive work for a living, the economic value of your labor is heading toward zero and potentially negative. 
Humans are about to become the slowest, least reliable members of the team. And there is no market for negative value labor.
So when you ask "how quickly can organizations transition before it is too late," the answer is: three years. 
By 2028, the patterns will be set. 
Organizations that have not shifted to NEO thinking will find it nearly impossible to catch up (not impossible). 
The compound advantage gap will be too large to bridge.
This is why Cotruglian philosophy is not merely desirable but structurally necessary.
 In Networked, Exponential, and Orchestrated environments, the old extraction strategies fail faster. 
Patient building strategies compound faster. 
Virtue-based coordination we believe is a sustainable operating system because AI commoditizes knowledge while creating a premium for human judgment, trust, and ethical coordination.
The transition question has two parts. 
First, can organizations make this shift? 
Yes, if they start now. 
The frameworks exist. The case studies exist (Haier, Bayer, others). 
The development pathways are documented.
Second, will they? 
Most will not. 
Most will continue running Industrial or Information Era logic until it is too late. 
They will dismiss the need in Years 1 and 2. 
They will see the gap become visible in Year 3. By Year 4 and 5, compound effects lock in. 
By Year 6, they are either benefiting from the compound advantages of transformation or suffering the compound consequences of stagnation.
The Philosophy Lab exists precisely for this reason. 
Not to discuss whether this philosophy is desirable. 
But to evaluate it, practice it, evolve it, and prepare people to lead the transition while there is still time.
Because trends and world are changing really fast, so if company wants to keep up, transition and keeping up the pace is essential
Why would it ever be to late to transition?

Reading through the introduction and reflections, it’s clear how deeply the Cotruglian approach aligns with the challenges and opportunities of today’s NEO Era. I appreciate the careful balance between historical insight, practical evidence, and stress-testing the philosophy—acknowledging that even trust-based systems can be corrupted while showing how they can outperform extractive models in networked, transparent, and AI-augmented environments. The lab’s focus on building transition frameworks, turning ethics into competitive advantage, and moving from theory to real-world payoff makes this not just an intellectual exercise but a blueprint for actionable leadership. Excited to engage with this discipline, test assumptions, and explore what building-oriented leadership can achieve in practice.

We’re not here to debate history. We’re here to use it.
To become wiser, tougher, more impactful leaders, for our families, our teams, our communities, and the future we’re shaping.

We move from theory to practice, from philosophy to payoff.

3. We can’t change history, but we can seek to understand trajectory.
You’ve put it plainly and powerfully: the goal isn’t to lament the “wrong turn,” but to build the next turn better.
This lab is about course correction with tools that work—not in theory, but in our world, in our markets, in your leadership reality.
We’re here to mine history for practical insight, not nostalgia.
To turn wisdom into resilience, antifragility, impact—and yes, MONEY.
Because doing well and doing good shouldn’t be a trade-off.
They should be the same strategy.
Looking forward to building that playbook with you.

2. You’ve touched on the deepest layer: context.
Machiavellian strategies dont win by acciden. They operate in current scarcity-driven, zero-sum, low-transparency environments.
And you’re right: sheepdogs can turn feral.
Every philosophy can be weaponized. Every system can be gamed.
That’s why this lab won’t be a praise session for Cotrugli—it will be a stress test.
We’ll ask: How do you keep trust from becoming tribalism? How do you guard against ethics as performance? How do you lead with openness without being naive?
This is the kind of thinking we need—not just adopting a new model, but building the antibodies against its corruption.
Your skepticism is welcome here.

1.You’ve captured the core tension perfectly. How fast can organizations transition?—is exactly what we’ll pressure-test in the lab.
We won’t just admire the philosophy; we’ll build transition frameworks. Ways to move from wolf systems to sheepdog systems without losing competitive edge.
Because in the end, it’s not about choosing ethics over profit.
It’s about realizing that in the NEO era, ethics could become competitive advantage.

I read all the information and detail shared to date. From my side it’s clear that my management style, culture and personality aligns with Cotruglain leadership approach. The point that I am contemplating is, we cannot change history so even if it’s a wrong turn or wrong decision that was made 100’s of years ago. It happened and we must live with it, the bigger point is we need to course correct and that is the important part currently.

2.The structural advantages of trust-based approaches in networked environments seem plausible: trust might accelerate coordination, reputation could compound into competitive advantages, and clear values might enable more effective AI alignment. But whether Cotruglian philosophy is immune to "corruption" (the sheepdug turning feral), remains an open question to me. It seems possible that when organizations operating under "trusted" sheepdog philosophy accumulate significant power, they might begin to use that philosophy as justification for behaviors that look more like extraction than value creation. The network effects and reputation that enable coordination could potentially become tools for exclusion and monopoly. The sheepdog still has teeth, and a trusted philosophy might become a shield for extraction when left unchecked.
1. The open question though, is not whether Cotruglian leadership is desirable, but how quickly companies, organizations and governments can transition before it’s too late.

While reading this after just finishing Peter Thiel's Zero to One, I had some thoughts that might be worth delving into a bit deeper.

There's a question of why Machiavellian approaches dominated for so long. Perhaps they weren't just successful (narative war) but actually necessary in certain contexts: situations of existential threat, zero-sum competition, or rapid disruption where trust-based coordination might have been too slow or fragile. This suggesting that different conditions might favor different philosophies, and that what looks like a "wrong turn" from one perspective might have been the only viable path from another (This could very well be the effect of winning the narative war, but maybe there is something more to it). The modern shift toward Cotruglian approaches does make sense because business conditions have and are fundamentally changing. Geographic dispersed colaboration, transparent networks, reputation economies, and AI orchestration might have recreated something closer to the medieval trading environment where Cotrugli's principles first emerged.

The structural advantages of trust-based approaches in networked environments seem plausible: trust might accelerate coordination, reputation could compound into competitive advantages, and clear values might enable more effective AI alignment. But whether Cotruglian philosophy is immune to "corruption" (the sheepdug turning feral), remains an open question to me. It seems possible that when organizations operating under "trusted" sheepdog philosophy accumulate significant power, they might begin to use that philosophy as justification for behaviors that look more like extraction than value creation. The network effects and reputation that enable coordination could potentially become tools for exclusion and monopoly. The sheepdog still has teeth, and a trusted philosophy might become a shield for extraction when left unchecked.

Dear Vanguards, I hope you are well. I am looking for a consulting firm that I could partner with in the field of result based financing independent verification (auditing) for energy access projects.  In Malawi, the WorldBank program, ASCENT has released a request for Expression of Interest for a consultancy services for a  Result based financing (RBF) independent verification Agent related to clean cooking, and solar home systems (products). The consultancy assignment  is expected to run for 3 years. EOI is due 9th March 2026. I have the technical expertise in the field, but lacking relevant financial management experience ( IVA contracts minimum USD250000), so looking for a consulting firm that I could support or work under. What i would offer is my local market expertise (on the key topics), support identification of local experts and support the development of the proposal, and would be able to offer on the ground support. Incase there might be any interested in this, do inbox me.

Call Title: Improving Climate and Weather Models for Africa Objective: The European Commission is inviting grant applications to strengthen weather and climate modelling in Africa to support more effective adaptation, disaster risk management, and capacity building. Eligible Activities: The project focuses on improving the quality and performance of weather and climate models tailored to Africa’s needs, enhancing the provision of weather predictions and climate projections to enable communities to respond to climate impacts, and bolstering African climate research to reduce reliance on external expertise, increase representation in international climate bodies, and contribute to global climate initiatives. Africa faces high vulnerability to climate change due to limited adaptive capacity, and despite global advancements in climate science, many benefits remain underutilized on the continent. This initiative seeks to address knowledge gaps in weather and climate modelling specific to Africa, improving the understanding and representation of regional, interregional, and continental climate dynamics. It aims to enhance model predictive skill and reduce uncertainties in rainfall and extreme weather events by leveraging observational data to advance process-based understanding across land, ocean, and atmospheric systems. Improved data collection, assimilation, and quality assurance, as well as integration of local and rescued historical datasets, are central to providing high-quality model inputs. Digital technologies, including AI and machine learning, as well as high-performance computing, are encouraged to support these objectives.
Research efforts are expected to be conducted in close collaboration between African and European teams to ensure relevance, co-ownership, and acceptance of solutions by African stakeholders. Capacity building and knowledge exchange are essential to equip African researchers with the tools and expertise needed for sustainable progress. Strong partnerships with universities, research institutions, National Meteorological and Hydrological Services, and African Regional Climate Centers will facilitate mutual learning and collaboration.
The initiative also emphasizes synergies with other EU-supported programs in climate modelling and capacity development, such as Regional Centres of Excellence focused on the green transition. Facilitating access to European research infrastructures, including supercomputing resources and data repositories, will further strengthen institutional and regional climate expertise in Africa. Eligible Applicants: Any legal entity is eligible to participate, regardless of its place of establishment, including entities from non-associated third countries and international organisations such as international European research organisations, whether or not they are eligible for funding, provided that they meet the conditions set out in   18
  the Horizon Europe Regulation along with any additional requirements specified in the relevant call or topic.
Total Budget: €21,000,000
Deadline: 15/04/2026

Call Title: Entries open for VinFuture Prize Objective: The VinFuture Prize honors scientific research and breakthrough technological innovations that can make a significant difference in the daily lives of millions of people.
The VinFuture Prizes include a US$3 million Grand Prize and three US$500,000 Special Prizes that honour breakthrough scientific and technological innovations with proven or potential real-world impact aligned with the United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goals. Eligible Activities: The VinFuture Special Prize for Innovators with Outstanding Achievements in Emerging Fields recognises work in non-traditional and interdisciplinary areas where multiple disciplines merge to form new fields, such as Quantum Computing, Bioelectronics, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Robotics, Internet of Things, Biometrics, Quantum Biology and Bioengineering.
The VinFuture Prize will partner with prestigious academic institutions and organizations around the globe to identify the most outstanding nominations that create meaningful change in the everyday lives of millions of people. No self- nominations are accepted and only nominations submitted in English through the official online nomination system will be considered.
Both organizational or institutional nominators and individual nominators are eligible to submit nominations, including universities, research institutes, academies of science and technology, scientific associations, corporations, industries, innovation incubators and prominent individuals in relevant fields. The deadline for nomination submissions for the 2026 VinFuture Prizes is 2:00 PM, April 17th, 2026 (Vietnam Time, GMT +7). Nominations received after this deadline will be considered for the following year. Eligible Applicants: The awards are open to individual researchers or teams involved in developing scientifically validated solutions that have benefited or can benefit, millions of people globally, with special recognition for innovators from developing countries, women innovators and innovators working in emerging and interdisciplinary fields.
The VinFuture Special Prize for Women Innovators is awarded only to women researchers or innovators, with no restriction on nationality, age, social status or economic background of the nominees, highlighting their significant contributions to science, technology and innovation.   19
 Total Budget: $3,000,000
Deadline: 17/04/2026

https://worldbusinessmuseum.com  
 It is believed that the true architects of our world are not those who rule by fear, but those who built the networks of trust that allow strangers to trade and collaborate on a global scale.
Machiavelli is often quoted because his philosophy is a weapon for wolves navigating a world of sheep. 
But we quote Cotrugli because we believe in a different project altogether: not just navigating the world, but building a better one. 
You are not just playing the game; you are quietly, diligently, improving the rules.
The world will never forget the name of the man who taught princes how to break promises. 
It falls to a rare few, like you, to remember the man who taught merchants why they should keep them. 

And in the long run, it is the builders, not the princes, who lay the foundation for everything that truly lasts.

Cotruglian philosophy is appreciated in a ratio of less than 1.000.000/1 
Our story begins, as it often does, with the famous one. 
Picture Machiavelli in his study, a man exiled and embittered.
 He has been tortured, his political career shattered. From this cauldron of resentment and genius, he pours forth The Prince. 
It is not a book of lofty ideals; it is a surgeon’s manual for a dying patient—the Italian state. 
His prose is a cold, sharp blade. He speaks of lions and foxes, of whether it is better to be loved or feared, and famously advises that a ruler must "learn how not to be good."
His world is a grand, bloody theater. The stage is the palace, the characters are princes and popes, and the stakes are the life or death of nations. It is dramatic, ruthless, and terrifyingly compelling. When you read Machiavelli, you feel you are being let in on a dark, intoxicating secret: this is how the world is really run. 
It’s no wonder he became one of the most quoted philosophers in history. Every ambitious CEO, every ruthless politician, every scheming character in a thriller leans on his words. He is the patron saint of realists, the philosopher of the necessary evil.
Now, let’s pull back the curtain on this grand stage and look beneath it. While Machiavelli was charting the currents of power in the prince’s court, Benedetto Cotrugli was in his counting house, doing something far less glamorous, but in his own way, he was leading his business-trade.
Cotrugli’s world was not the theater of the palace, but the workshop of civilization. 
His masterpiece, Della Mercatura e del Mercante Perfetto (On Merchandise and the Perfect Merchant), is not about seizing power. 
It is about building it—brick by brick, ledger by ledger. 
He argued that the perfect merchant must be honest, literate, pious, and that his word must be his bond. 
For Cotrugli, like Aristotle, virtue was not a weakness; it was the bedrock of credit. A good name was a more valuable asset than a full treasury. 
His focus was on creating systems—reliable accounting, trustworthy networks, sustainable practices—that would enable commerce and, thus, society to flourish.
So, why does everyone know Machiavelli’s name, while Cotrugli remains a ghost?
It’s because we are mesmerized by the loud power, not the silent art. 
Machiavelli writes about the dramatic moment when the dagger is plunged.
Cotrugli writes about the thousand quiet days of trade that built the city where the assassination happens. 
We are hardwired to pay attention to the killer, not the trader; to the conqueror, not the merchants who fund his army.
Machiavelli gives us a thrilling, single-player game of thrones. 
His lessons are about individual brilliance, cunning, and force of will.
They are quotable because they are shocking and absolve us of the burden of morality. Quoting Machiavelli makes you look tough and shrewd.
Cotrugli, on the other hand, gives us the multiplayer game of civilization. His lessons are about cooperation, trust, and long-term investment. 
They are about the unsexy, essential plumbing of prosperity: accurate ledgers, fulfilled contracts, and a reputation for honesty. These are not topics that make for gripping cocktail-party quotes. 
They are the slow, patient work of building a world, not conquering one.
And this is where your role comes in. To champion Cotrugli is to make a profound and contrarian choice. It is to argue that the most potent force in history is not the sword of the prince, but the ledger of the merchant or business in general. 
War and Business are the most potent forces that shape our history, present, and future.

And saying that, think about the way there are hundreds of war museums in the world and just a few about business.


The first one in the world is the COTRUGLI Business Museum, and we will dedicate a few stories in future  on how and why we created it.

https://cotrugli.si/museum/biographies

Prince or Trader 
Imagine two men born in the same century. 
Both were sharp-eyed realists who looked at the chaotic world around them and decided to write a manual for success. 
One became a global icon, his name a byword for political cunning. 
The other vanished into the footnotes of history, known and respected only to a handful of specialists and COTRUGLI alumni.

This is the story of Niccolò Machiavelli and Benedetto Cotrugli—the philosopher of power and philosopher of virtue and trade as an Art.
Machiavellian philosophy is appreciated by many.

Hi

At COTRUGLI Business School, we hold these principles in high regard and infuse them throughout our educational portfolio. Our curriculum and approach are designed to instill a deep sense of ethics, integrity, and responsible leadership in our alums.  
 
What makes us unique? 
 
We are passionate and love what we do. We are not just about our people (Alumni students, employers, professionals, clients) but all people and the planet. For more than two decades, we have been living the case that caring for people and the planet brings pride, joy, and well-deserved profit. 
 
AMBA Accreditation: COTRUGLI is accredited by the Association of MBAs (AMBA), distinguishing it among the top 2% of business schools globally. This accreditation underscores our commitment to high educational standards and international recognition. 
 
Top Faculty: The school boasts a loyal faculty of academics and experts teaching who are actively engaged in leading business schools and consulting for global companies. 30% of our faculty has been with us for 20 years. 
 
Strong Alumni Network: With over 20,000 leaders and managers passing through our programs and 2700+ Alumni, the school offers excellent networking opportunities, connecting current students with Alum network. 
 
HashNet: COTRUGLI's tech arm specializes in social innovation and frontier tech, such as Blockchain, AI, VR, XR, and Metaverse. Sistership with HashNet enhances COTRUGLI's ability to provide an education that prepares students for VUCA challenges and opportunities. 
 
European Digital Innovation Hub: Joining the European Digital Innovation Hub signifies COTRUGLI's active participation in a network that supports digital transformation and innovation across Europe. This affiliation provides students and faculty access to cutting-edge resources and collaboration opportunities in digital projects, further enriching the learning environment and positioning the school as a digital innovation and practice leader. 
 
 
SEED for Social Innovation and AIM Global with the UN: COTRUGLI's involvement in SEED for Social Innovation and its partnership with AIM Global under the United Nations highlight our commitment to social entrepreneurship. These initiatives highlight our dedication to leveraging business education for social impact and using AI for the public good. 
 
Museum: COTRUGLI is home to the First business museum in the world; this unique educational resource showcases the history of business and serves as a source of inspiration, illustrating its evolution. 
 
CO-LAB: A space where our Alumni, students, and the broader community can collaborate, experiment, iterate, share resources, and develop solutions, regardless of location. 
 
These aspects of COTRUGLI Business School enhance our unique standing by integrating business education with frontier technologies, innovation, history, and social responsibility, offering the COTRUGLI approach to business education. 
 
COTRUGLI in numbers 
 
20.000+ participants engaged in our business educational programs in SE Europe. 
3-degree programs:  
Master of Business Administration (MBA) 
Executive Master of Business Administration (EMBA) 
Chief Executive Master of Business Administration (CEMBA) 
2700+ students in MBA degree programs 
 
Two campuses: Zagreb, Belgrade 
Branch offices: Bulgaria, Montenegro, Romania, Slovenia  
17 + nationalities:  
38% of female MBA participants 
62% of male MBA participants

COTRUGLI Business School is a private boutique business school that offers three MBA programs accredited by AMBA: the MBA program, the Executive MBA program, and the Chief Executive MBA program. The Executive MBA program was launched in 2003 in Zagreb and later introduced in Belgrade in 2008. The MBA and Chief Executive MBA programs began in 2009. All programs have maintained a consistent accreditation record, receiving initial accreditation for five years in 2009 and re-accredited in 2014, 2019, and 2024 for additional five-year periods.  
 
 
CO lab is established in 2024 as a spin-off of COTRUGLI Business School, leveraging COTRUGLI’s 20+ years of educational expertise and robust alumni network. Headquartered in the USA and EU, CO lab operates across multiple regions, including the Middle East and Africa. CO lab is legally structured as Deleware LLC with plans to scale globally through strategic partnerships and targeted expansions. 
 
Mission and Vision 
● Mission: To empower individuals and organizations with purpose-driven education, enhancing leadership and entrepreneurial skills to foster personal, corporate, and societal growth. CO lab is committed to creating a positive social impact by promoting accessible education for underserved communities. 
● Vision: To award 1.000,000 MBA scholarships in developing countries over the next ten years, beginning with 300 scholarships in 2025, supporting a new generation of leaders who drive sustainable progress. 
Core Values 
● Accessibility: Ensuring education is available to all, regardless of financial background. 
● Innovation: Integrating AI and blockchain to provide cutting-edge, flexible, and secure learning experiences. 
● Community Impact: Developing a network that supports education, mentorship, and practical skill-building with a focus on social responsibility. 
● Collaboration: Fostering a collaborative ecosystem where alumni and learners connect, share knowledge, and drive professional growth. 
Goals and Objectives 
1. Establish CO lab as a Leading EdTech Platform: Develop and scale AI-driven educational programs tailored to individual learning needs and industry requirements. 
2. Expand CO lab’s Consultant Network: Grow the alumni-driven consulting network to serve corporate clients and foster impactful professional connections. 
3. Achieve Sustainable Growth through B2B Partnerships: Strengthen corporate alliances to provide high-quality, customizable training solutions for businesses across industries. 
4. Maximize Social Impact: Increase the annual number of scholarships offered and align education programs with UN Sustainable Development Goals. 
Past Accomplishments 
● Strategic Partnerships with organizations like the UN and EU, validating CO lab’s innovative approach to social impact education. 
● High Customer Satisfaction during 20 years of COTRUGLI legacy with a Net Promoter Score (NPS) of 9.5. 
● COTRUGLI alumni network through the CO lab, bringing together 2,700 alumni professionals who provide mentorship and consulting services. 
Inspiration 
 
At COTRUGLI Business School, a unique blend of timeless wisdom and forward-thinking defines our institution. In recognition of Benedetto Cotrugli’s profound impact, we have named our School after him. His career encompassed the roles of merchant, humanist, scientist, and diplomat. One of Cotrugli's remarkable achievements was the authorship of "The Art of Trade" in 1458, a book with the first exposition of double-entry bookkeeping principles and methods. This contribution to accounting marks his place in history. However, his book extended beyond accounting; it delved into personal development and highlighted the value of ethical conduct in business. These timeless principles and ethical business practices drove our decision to honor Cotrugli by naming our school after him.

Netflix just made the most DANGEROUS move in Hollywood.
They didn't buy Warner Bros but instead bought something far more threatening.
An AI company, built in secret and founded by one of Hollywood's own.

Ben Affleck.

The company is called InterPositive and you've never heard of it. 
And that was the point. 
Affleck quietly registered it under a shell company called Fin Bone LLC. 

He filed patents under his legal name. 

He built a proprietary AI dataset on a closed soundstage, all while making movies.

Here's what InterPositive does. 

It takes the raw footage from a film shoot, the dailies and trains a custom AI model on it.

That model can then relight scenes, reframe shots, remove stunt wires, fix missing angles, color correct entire films and add visual effects.

Work that currently employs thousands of people in post production.

Netflix now owns all of it, exclusively and no other studio gets access.

Think about the timing.

One week ago, Netflix walked away from an $83 billion bid to buy Warner Bros. 

Wall Street cheered and the stock jumped 14%.

Days later, they quietly acquired the technology that could make traditional studios obsolete.

They just bought Hollywood's replacement.

And they made Ben Affleck an Oscar winner, a director, a guy people trust, the face of it.

SAG-AFTRA's contract expires in June. 

AI is the single biggest issue on the table and the last fight over AI shut Hollywood down for months in 2023.

And Netflix just acquired an AI production company, right before negotiations.

They're calling it tools for filmmakers and they say it empowers storytellers.

That's what they always say.

The real question, how many jobs disappear when a machine can do in seconds what a VFX team does in weeks?

Netflix won't answer that but the technology already did
Tough but honest decision
And smart and fast eat slow
They are faster and smarter then most of companies.

On the other side...
